description: Natural function definition syntax. Define functions using familiar math notation. Supports scalar, point-returning, and vector-returning functions. Functions can reference and compose other user-defined functions. Derivative notation with primes is supported.
utility: Define mathematical functions using natural notation (e.g., f(x) = sin(x))

arguments.name: body
arguments.description: Function body — scalar, point tuple (x, y), or vector {x, y}

alternates.description: Returns a 2D point tuple — use with point(G, f(t))
Returns a 2D vector — use with vector expressions
Evaluate the derivative of f at x=1
Define a transformed function. The parameter expression (x+1) is substituted into the body. g(x) = 3(x+1)+1, which is f(x) shifted left by 1.
Define a horizontally compressed function. The parameter expression (2x) is substituted into the body.
Define a piecewise function with alternating expression-condition pairs and an optional default value at the end. The function is callable everywhere: plot, table, arithmetic.

examples.expression:
f(t) = (3cos(t), 3sin(t))
P = point(G, f(0))
Q = point(G, f(pi/4), icon(cross))
R = point(G, f(pi/2))

f(x) = sin(x)
g(x) = cos(x)
h(x) = f(x) + g(x)

f(x, y) = x^2 + y^2
P = plot3d(G, f, range(-3, 3), range(-3, 3))

examples.scenario: Function transformation — compare f(x) and f(x+1)
examples.expression:
f(x) = 3x+1
g(x+1) = 3(x+1)+1
graph_1 = g2d(at(1, 2), 19, 25, range(-5, 5), range(-10, 10), type(plotting))
plot_1 = plot(graph_1, f, c(teal))
graph_2 = g2d(at(1, 30), 19, 25, range(-5, 5), range(-10, 10), type(plotting))
plot_2 = plot(graph_2, g, c(red))

examples.scenario: Piecewise function with table and plot
examples.expression:
f(t) = piecewise("1", "t >= -0.5 and t <= 0.5", "0")
w=3
g(t)="f(t)*cos(wt)"
graph_1=g2d(at(10.5,1),19,25,range(-2,2),range(-2,2,0.5),type(plotting))
T=table(at(1.2,19.8),"t","f(t)","g(t)",range(-2,2,0.5))
plottable_1 = plottable(graph_1, T, 2, c(teal))
plottable_2 = plottable(graph_1, T, 3, c(teal))

f(x) = piecewise("x+2", "x < -1", "1", "x >= -1 and x <= 1", "x^2", "x > 1")
graph_1 = g2d(at(4, 4), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 10), type(plotting))
plot_1 = plot(graph_1, f, c(blue))

notes: Converts natural syntax into FunctionDefinitionExpression internally
Automatically inlines referenced user-defined functions in the body
Point-returning functions use parenthesized tuple: f(t) = (x_expr, y_expr)
Vector-returning functions use curly braces: v(t) = {x_expr, y_expr}
Function transformation: f(expr) = body substitutes the parameter expression into the body. f(x-5) = x defines f(x) = (x-5), a horizontal shift right by 5.
Piecewise functions: f(x) = piecewise("expr1", "cond1", ..., "default") defines a callable piecewise function usable in plot, table, and arithmetic.
